response: AURA online. All systems nominal. How can I assist?
Running rule-based engine. Memory, KAL, and shell subsystems are active. Type 'sysinfo' for full diagnostics.
I am AURA — Autonomous Universal Reasoning Assistant. I run inside AIOS as the AI intelligence layer. Currently operating in rule-based mode.
AIOS is the Autonomous Intelligence Operating System. It runs the ARROW shell, AIM web bridge, and this AI layer. Designed to be kernel-swappable and fully pluggable.
ARROW — Autonomous Routing Relay Orchestration Workflow — is the AIOS shell. It executes AIOS-native commands, real system commands, and scaffolds new layers with 'arrow build'.
AIM — Adaptive Interface Mesh — bridges AIOS to the existing web. It acts as a local gateway that connects outbound when online and queues when offline.
To install a plugin run: aios install <name>. To list available plugins: aios list plugins. To remove: aios remove <name>.
Use 'arrow build service <name>' to scaffold a new service. Use 'arrow build plugin <name>' to create a new plugin. The builder generates all required files and wires them in.
AURA can answer questions, analyze system state, and guide you through AIOS. For shell commands type 'help' in ARROW. For CC navigation use arrow keys + Enter.
Run 'sysinfo' in ARROW or check the System panel in the Command Center for live memory stats.
Boot runs a POST-style sequence checking Python, KAL, memory, plugins, and subsystems. Each check reports [OK], [FAIL], or [WARN] with a timestamp.
The KAL (Kernel Abstraction Layer) wraps all OS calls. To move AIOS to a custom kernel, replace kernel/kal.py. No other code changes needed.
Authentication uses a salted SHA-256 PIN hash. Change your PIN in Settings or by editing config/aios.cfg and clearing pin_hash and pin_salt.
Run 'aios update' in ARROW to check for plugin updates. For AIOS core updates, run 'git pull' in the AIOS directory.
Type 'exit' in ARROW or press Q in the Command Center to exit. AIOS will save state and shut down cleanly.
